conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on the actual diameter
paper_title: Small diameters and generators for arithmetic lattices in $\mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{R})$ and certain Ramanujan graphs
summary: Steiner proves that the almost-diameter of $\Gamma\backslash\mathbb{H}$ is at most $(2+o(1))\log 3V_\Gamma$ (Theorem 3), and remarks that this bound is sharp; the conjecture that the actual diameter is of the same order $(2+o(1))\log 3V_\Gamma$ remains speculation. A search of Steiner's subsequent publication list and the literature through May 2026 found no paper resolving this informal conjecture.
search_queries: Steiner arithmetic lattices SL2 diameter hyperbolic surface 2207.12684
diameter hyperbolic surface arithmetic lattice "almost diameter" sharp bound generators Frobenius norm
Steiner "actual diameter" hyperbolic surface arithmetic lattice "log V" conjecture cite 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture was found. Steiner's own post-2022 work moves to Kloosterman sums and sup-norm problems, with no return to the diameter problem. The conjecture is informal (a speculation in the text) rather than a formal numbered conjecture, making it less likely to attract direct citation. The almost-diameter bound $(2+o(1))\log 3V_\Gamma$ is proved and sharp; whether the true diameter matches this order is open.
